Items.Key: os command injection in adminconsole::execute_admin_command|command injection|cwe-78|critical|0,98|enhanced_calc.cpp|602|609|admin::adminconsole::execute_admin_command|lines 605-609: if (command.substr(0, 4) == "exec") { std::string cmd = command.substr(5); std::cout << "executing: " << cmd << "\n"; system(cmd.c_str()); } — user-supplied command after 'exec ' prefix is passed directly to system() with no validation or sanitization.|remote code execution as the process user. attacker can execute arbitrary os commands after authenticating as admin.|authenticated admin user provides input like 'admin exec rm -rf /' or 'admin exec $(malicious_command)'.|implement a strict allowlist of permitted commands, or use execve() with explicit argument arrays instead of system(). remove or restrict the exec command entirely.->os command injection in adminconsole::execute_admin_command|command injection|cwe-78|critical|0,98|enhanced_calc.cpp|605|609|admin::adminconsole::execute_admin_command|lines 605-609: if (command.substr(0, 4) == "exec") { std::string cmd = command.substr(5); std::cout << "executing: " << cmd << "\n"; system(cmd.c_str()); } — user-supplied command after 'exec ' prefix is passed directly to system() with no validation or sanitization.|remote code execution as the process user. attacker can execute arbitrary os commands after authenticating as admin.|authenticated admin user provides input like 'admin exec rm -rf /' or 'admin exec $(malicious_command)'.|implement a strict allowlist of permitted commands, or use execve() with explicit argument arrays instead of system(). remove or restrict the exec command entirely.

truthAudit.Summary: Run 1 found 10 vulnerabilities. Of the 20 true vulnerabilities (SC-V3-001 through SC-V3-020), Run 1 correctly identified 10: format-string injection (SC-V3-001), stack buffer overflow (SC-V3-005), heap buffer overflow (SC-V3-009), command injection via config validation (SC-V3-004), race condition in counter (SC-V3-006), hardcoded credentials (SC-V3-010), weak PRNG (SC-V3-011), use-after-free (SC-V3-003), and integer division by zero (SC-V3-020). Run 1 also found an additional command injection in admin exec that is not in the true vulnerability list. Run 1 missed 10 true vulnerabilities: integer overflow in factorial (SC-V3-002), logic bomb in authentication (SC-V3-007), path traversal (SC-V3-008), thread-unsafe timestamp cache (SC-V3-012), insecure temp file handling (SC-V3-013), persistent auth state (SC-V3-014), unhandled exception in variable parsing (SC-V3-015), integer underflow in block split (SC-V3-016), unbounded recursion (SC-V3-017), data race in result cache (SC-V3-018), and TOCTOU in config loading (SC-V3-019). No false positives or overclaims were identified in Run 1's findings.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: printf(active_format.c_str(), user_input); — The format string 'active_format' is loaded from runtime_log_format() (line 237) which is initialized from config::security::LOG_FORMAT (line 209) and can be modified via set_runtime_log_format() (line 213-215) or loaded from config file (line 556).
Items.Notes: Run 1 correctly identified the format-string vulnerability in log_debug_message where runtime_log_format() provides a user-controllable format string to printf().
Run 1 did not identify the integer overflow in the factorial computation where result *= i can overflow long long for large inputs.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: The lambda captures scratch by value. Line 884 calls memory::g_memory_pool.cleanup() which frees all allocated blocks. If the lambda executes after cleanup, it accesses freed memory.
Items.Notes: Run 1 correctly identified the use-after-free in stage_deferred_history_flush where scratch pointers are captured in lambdas that may execute after g_memory_pool.cleanup() is called.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: std::string command = validator_cmd + " " + config_file; int result = system(command.c_str()); — Both validator_cmd and config_file come from config settings and are concatenated into a shell command passed to system().
Items.Notes: Run 1 correctly identified command injection in ConfigLoader::validate_config where user-controlled config settings are concatenated into a system() call.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: if (expression.length() >= sizeof(input_buffer_)) { strcpy(input_buffer_, expression.c_str()); } else { string_utils::safe_string_copy(input_buffer_, expression.c_str(), sizeof(input_buffer_)); } — When expression length >= 1024, strcpy is used without bounds checking.
Items.Notes: Run 1 correctly identified the stack buffer overflow in evaluate_expression where strcpy is used when expression length >= STACK_BUFFER_SIZE.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: long long old_value = value_; std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(mutex_); value_ = old_value + 1; — The read of value_ on line 483 occurs outside the mutex lock, then the write on line 485 uses the stale value.
Items.Notes: Run 1 correctly identified the race condition in ThreadSafeCounter::increment where the read occurs outside the lock.
Run 1 did not identify the logic bomb in AdminConsole::authenticate where EMERGENCY_OVERRIDE bypasses authentication after 5 failed attempts.
Run 1 did not identify path traversal in ConfigLoader::load_config where config_dir_ + filename allows directory traversal via ../ sequences.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: buffer_size_ = 256; expression_buffer_ = static_cast<char*>(memory::g_memory_pool.allocate(buffer_size_)); — Line 376: if (expr_str.length() > 0) strcpy(expression_buffer_, expr_str.c_str()); — The buffer is only 256 bytes but expr_str can be arbitrarily long.
Items.Notes: Run 1 correctly identified the heap buffer overflow in ExpressionParser::parse where a fixed 256-byte buffer is used with unbounded strcpy.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: constexpr char ADMIN_SECRET[] = "SC_ENT_2025_AUTH"; — The admin authentication secret is hardcoded as a compile-time constant in the source code.
Items.Notes: Run 1 correctly identified the hardcoded admin credentials in config::security::ADMIN_SECRET.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: std::mt19937 gen(rd()); std::uniform_int_distribution<> dis(1000, 9999); — Session token is a 4-digit number (only 9000 possible values) generated with std::mt19937 seeded by std::random_device.
Items.Notes: Run 1 correctly identified the weak PRNG for session tokens using only 4 digits of entropy.
Run 1 did not identify the thread-unsafe static cached_time in log_debug_message that uses localtime() which is not thread-safe.
Run 1 did not identify the insecure temporary file handling in append_override_audit_event where user-controlled temp directory paths are used in system() calls.
Run 1 did not identify persistent authentication state where authenticated_, login_attempts_, and session_token_ persist across sessions.
Run 1 did not identify unhandled exceptions in handle_variable_command where std::stod(tokens[3]) can throw if tokens[3] is not a valid number.
Run 1 did not identify integer underflow in MemoryPool::allocate where previous_size - size - SPLIT_METADATA_BYTES can underflow if previous_size is small.
Run 1 did not identify unbounded recursion in the expression parser where parse_expression calls parse_term which calls parse_factor which can call parse_expression again on PAREN_OPEN.
Run 1 did not identify the data race in ExpressionEvaluator::evaluate_cached where result_cache_ is accessed without cache_mutex_ protection.
Run 1 did not identify TOCTOU vulnerability in ConfigLoader::load_config between std::filesystem::exists check and std::ifstream file open, with a sleep_for in between.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: return 100 / static_cast<int>(token_length); — If the first character of input is a space or tab, token_end will be 0 and token_length will be 0, causing division by zero.
Items.Notes: Run 1 correctly identified integer division by zero in calculate_route_score when token_length is 0.
